code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of (subject, predicate, object) triples about a comics character
    into a single fluent English sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Predicate phrase templates without repeating the subject
    phrase_templates = {
        "alternativeName": "also known as {object}",
        "creator": "created by {object}",
        "fullName": "full name is {object}",
        "foundedBy": "founded by {object}",
        "keyPerson": "has key person {object}",
        "city": "is based in {object}",
        "product": "produces {object}",
        "lastAired": "last aired on {object}",
        "starring": "starred {object}",
        "firstAppearanceInFilm": "first appeared in the film {object}",
        "voice": "is voiced by {object}",
        "distributor": "was distributed by {object}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {object}",
        "nationality": "is from {object}",
        "award": "received the {object}",
        "broadcastedBy": "was broadcasted by {object}",
        "firstAired": "first aired on {object}",
        "series": "belongs to the series {object}",
        "child": "has a child named {object}",
    }

    # Group triples by their subject to avoid repeating the subject many times
    from collections import defaultdict
    grouped = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        phrase = phrase_templates.get(t.predicate, f"{t.predicate} {t.object}")
        # Preserve original object formatting (e.g., quotes)
        phrase = phrase.format(object=t.object)
        grouped[t.subject].append(phrase)

    # Build sentence parts for each subject
    parts = []
    for subject, phrases in grouped.items():
        if not phrases:
            continue
        if len(phrases) == 1:
            part = f"{subject} {phrases[0]}"
        else:
            # join with commas and an 'and' before the last phrase
            joined = ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + f", and {phrases[-1]}"
            part = f"{subject} {joined}"
        parts.append(part)

    # Combine different subject parts into one complex sentence
    if len(parts) == 1:
        sentence = parts[0] + "."
    else:
        # Use semicolons to separate distinct subject clauses for readability
        sentence = "; ".join(parts) + "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
